title: Dunhuang Manuscripts of the Heart Sūtra
description: Manuscripts of the Heart Sūtra discovered in the Dunhuang cave library (Cave 17, Mogao Caves). Sealed ca. 1000 CE and discovered in 1900, these manuscripts preserve early medieval copies of the text, some predating the Fangshan Stele (661 CE).

discovery.location: Cave 17 (Library Cave), Mogao Caves, Dunhuang, Gansu Province, China
discovery.significance: The Dunhuang library contained approximately 50,000 manuscripts, paintings, and textiles, sealed for nearly a millennium. The Heart Sūtra manuscripts provide crucial early witnesses.

beijing.name: Beijing Collection
beijing.notes: Manuscripts remaining in China after Stein and Pelliot acquisitions

other.note: Smaller collections in St. Petersburg (Russia), Kyoto (Japan), and other locations

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 般若波羅蜜多心經

variants: Minor orthographic variants typical of manuscript transmission

heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: One of the earlier Heart Sūtra manuscripts from Dunhuang. Text closely follows T251 tradition.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 般若波羅蜜多心經
heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: Complete copy of the Heart Sūtra from the Pelliot collection.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 般若波羅蜜多心經
heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: Heart Sūtra in booklet format, showing transition from scroll to codex.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 唐梵翻對字音般若波羅蜜多心經
heart_sutra_manuscripts.description: Bilingual version similar to T256, with Sanskrit transliterated in Chinese characters alongside Chinese translation.
heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: Important witness for Tang-era Sanskrit pronunciation. Confirms existence of Sanskrit text in Dunhuang.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 摩訶般若波羅蜜大明呪經
heart_sutra_manuscripts.description: Version corresponding to T250 (Dàmíngzhòujīng) rather than T251.
text_features: Uses 觀世音 (Guānshìyīn) rather than 觀自在 (Guānzìzài)
Uses 五陰 (wǔyīn) rather than 五蘊 (wǔyùn)
Three mantra epithets with 明呪
heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: This manuscript preserves the pre-Xuanzang tradition (T250-type), confirming the early existence of this version.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 般若波羅蜜多心經

dated_colophon.translation: Copied on the 20th day of the 7th month of the 5th year of Dazhong [851 CE]

heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: One of the few securely dated Dunhuang Heart Sūtra manuscripts. The date 851 CE provides a terminus ante quem for the T251 tradition at Dunhuang.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 般若波羅蜜多心經
heart_sutra_manuscripts.description: Heart Sūtra with interlinear commentary or glosses.
heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: Evidence of study and exegesis of the text at Dunhuang.

heart_sutra_manuscripts.title: 般若波羅蜜多心經
heart_sutra_manuscripts.notes: Heart Sūtra from the Beijing collection of Dunhuang manuscripts.

for_dating: P.2323 (dated 851 CE) confirms T251 tradition at Dunhuang by mid-9th century
S.700 (T250-type) confirms pre-Xuanzang tradition persisted alongside T251
Bilingual manuscripts (P.3917) confirm Sanskrit text existed by Tang period
for_textual_criticism: Dunhuang manuscripts generally follow T251 with minor orthographic variants
Presence of T250-type manuscripts confirms two distinct Chinese traditions
No manuscripts of Chinese long recension (T257-type) found, suggesting it was not known at Dunhuang
Bilingual texts confirm Sanskrit transliteration conventions
for_transmission: Dunhuang was a major Buddhist center on the Silk Road
Manuscripts show Heart Sūtra was widely copied for devotional and liturgical use
Both scroll and booklet formats attest to varied uses
Interlinear glosses show text was studied, not just recited

sample_text_s2464.note: Sample text from S.2464, showing typical Dunhuang manuscript orthography
sample_text_s2464.opening: 般若波羅蜜多心經。觀自在菩薩行深般若波羅蜜多時照見五蘊皆空度一切苦厄。舍利子色不異空空不異色色即是空空即是色受想行識亦復如是。
sample_text_s2464.comparison_t251: Essentially identical to T251 with minor orthographic variants

sample_text_s700_t250_type.note: Sample from S.700 showing T250-type readings
sample_text_s700_t250_type.opening: 摩訶般若波羅蜜大明呪經。觀世音菩薩行深般若波羅蜜時照見五陰皆空度一切苦厄。
distinctive_features: Title uses 大明呪經 (Great Illumination Mantra Sūtra)
觀世音 (Guānshìyīn) rather than 觀自在 (Guānzìzài)
五陰 (wǔyīn) rather than 五蘊 (wǔyùn)
